title: 'Talk without condition such as resignation': Govt to oppn leaders over NEET debate
content: The government is engaging opposition leaders to resolve the Parliament deadlock over the NEET paper leak.  Discussions aim to end disruptions which have stalled proceedings since the Monsoon session began.  The Centre is ready for debate without preconditions, including minister's resignation.  A meeting of floor leaders is likely soon to build consensus for the discussion.  This outreach follows repeated adjournments due to protests on examination irregularities.

title: '400 Pak handles identified': Delhi Police reveals Op Sindoor connection in NEET protests
content: Delhi Police identified and blocked over 400 Pakistani social media handles. These accounts are spreading misinformation and rumors amid ongoing protests. The same handles were active during Operation Sindoor and aim to provoke people. Police urged citizens, especially students, not to fall for fake content. Delhi Police respects citizens' right to peaceful protest and fact-checking.

title: 'Didn't want to live apart': 3 sisters marry their social media cameraman in UP's Amroha
content: Two sisters and their cousin married the same man, their social media cameraman. They chose this unusual union to avoid living separately after marriage. The sisters explained their decision stemmed from a desire to remain together always. The cameraman agreed to the marriage respecting their deep emotional bond. Legal experts state this marriage holds no recognition under Hindu law.

title: 'He is fearless': Shreyas hails Sooryavanshi after record-breaking blitz vs Zimbabwe
content: Teenage prodigy Vaibhav Sooryavanshi smashed a record fifty off nineteen balls. He became the youngest player to achieve this international milestone. India secured a seven-wicket victory in the opening T20I match. Captain Shreyas Iyer celebrated his first win as T20I skipper. The bowlers restricted Zimbabwe to 125 for seven runs.

title: 16-yr-old Canadian student built AI robot turtle that hunts plastic invisible to eye
content: Canadian teenager Evan Budz has built an autonomous AI robot turtle that swims on its own and detects microplastics in lakes with 94% accuracy. The 16-year-old spent roughly 4,000 hours on the bionic sea turtle and its underwater holographic camera, winning a $50,000 scholarship at the 2026 Regeneron ISEF. His goal: replace slow, expensive lab testing with on-the-spot microplastic detection in remote water bodies.

title: 'Failure is okay, cheating is not': Sachin Tendulkar speaks out on NEET protests
content: Sachin Tendulkar urged society to uphold hard work and meritocracy amid NEET exam protests. He stressed that adults must shape a culture valuing effort over shortcuts. Students' disappointment over unrewarded hard work is understandable and should be addressed. Other cricketers like Shikhar Dhawan and Mohammad Kaif also voiced concerns. They called for fair resolutions and an end to student mistreatment during protests.

title: Canada college to protesting Indian students: Seek legal advice, retain imp documents
content: Indian students protest after Portage College's business management diploma graduates faced work visa rejections.  The Immigration, Refugees and Citizenship Canada stated their programs were not eligible for permits.  Portage College encourages affected students to retain important documents and seek legal advice.  The college cannot overturn decisions made by the immigration authority.  Affected students should consult official government materials and obtain independent advice.

title: Premium petrol to remain ethanol-free; no plan to blend beyond 20%: Govt to Parliament
content: Premium petrol grades will continue to be ethanol-free for consumers.  The government has no plans to reintroduce lower ethanol blends like E0 or E10.  Current ethanol blending in regular petrol will remain at twenty percent.  No widespread adverse impacts have been reported from E20 fuel usage.  Future decisions on higher blending will follow extensive studies and consultations.

title: Drought-hit Chile community brings water back by rebuilding soil and trees
content: A rural community on Chile's Chiloe Island has restored its water supply after years of relying on emergency tanker trucks. By protecting forests, restoring its watershed, building a gravity-fed water network and involving local residents in maintenance, the community has maintained a reliable system for nearly seven years. The project is now being presented as a model for other rural areas facing water shortages.

title: She inherited her childhood home. Then a $228,000 water bill arrived
content: A woman in Baltimore, US, says she was shocked to discover a $228,000 unpaid water bill attached to her late father's home after inheriting the property. The debt has placed a lien on the house, preventing its sale. The dispute centres on whether the homeowner or the community's homeowners association should be responsible for the outstanding amount.

title: 50-year-old 'machine problem' is why The Odyssey can be seen in true IMAX at 41 theatres
content: Christopher Nolan's The Odyssey is available in limited 70mm IMAX theaters worldwide.  This scarcity stems from aging technology and the lack of new projector manufacturing.  IMAX must rebuild and retrofit its old machines, which are large and complex.  The film prints themselves are also massive, weighing hundreds of pounds.  This combination restricts screenings to only forty-one global locations.

title: DDA launches housing scheme with 25% discount: 1,200+ flats, starting from Rs 33 lakh
content: The Delhi Development Authority has launched a new housing scheme for government employees and professionals. This scheme offers over twelve hundred ready-to-move flats with a significant twenty-five percent discount. Registration for these affordable homes opens on July twenty-fourth and booking begins August fifteenth. Flats are located in Narela and will be allotted on a first come, first served basis.

title: IndiGo offers crisis management plan to settle CCI probe into flight disruption case
content: IndiGo has proposed commitments to the Competition Commission of India. These proposals aim to address concerns over past flight disruptions and unfair practices. The airline will establish a crisis management group and a passenger grievance mechanism. IndiGo also offered to temporarily hand over airport slots during major disruptions. The CCI is now seeking public comments on these proposed commitments.

title: NFL schedule 2026: Full Eagles, Chiefs, Cowboys and all 32 team schedules
content: Find the complete 2026 NFL schedule for all 32 teams, including Eagles, Chiefs, Cowboys and every franchise. See game dates, kickoff times, bye weeks, international matchups, prime-time games, TV channels and streaming options. Track Week 18 updates and late-season schedule changes in one easy-to-use guide for the entire regular season, from opening night through final playoff push all season long.

title: Marc Cucurella's untied hair hides an emotional promise to his autistic son
content: Marc Cucurella's long hair carries a deeply personal meaning. The Spain defender reportedly keeps it untied so his autistic son, Mateo, can easily recognize him on the pitch. Cucurella and partner Claudia Rodriguez have also shared their emotional journey after Mateo's autism diagnosis and how it transformed their family's life.

title: Why Wimbledon champion Linda Noskova asked, 'Why am I here?'
content: Linda Noskova, fresh from her Wimbledon title and ranked world No. 7, publicly questioned why sports outlet Outsports used her image to illustrate a story on the WTA's new genetic sex-testing policy. The WTA's updated Women's Eligibility Policy, effective Tuesday, mandates a one-time SRY gene screening for all players, replacing an older framework that permitted transgender women to compete under certain testosterone and gender declaration conditions. Screening begins in 2026. Noskova's "Why am I here?" comment drew significant reaction online, with fans questioning the editorial decision. The outlet has yet to respond.

title: CWG 2026: Putul Sonowal stuns world champion Ryan Bester in lawn bowls thriller
content: Putul Sonowal achieved a sensational upset against the reigning world champion in lawn bowls.  Rupa Rani Tirkey and Pinki Singh also survived a thriller in the Women's Pairs event.  Sonowal showed remarkable composure to defeat a decorated Canadian opponent in his debut.  The Indian women's pair secured victory after a tense tie-break against Malta.  These wins have raised hopes for India's lawn bowls campaign at the games.

title: Trump adds Israel strings to Saudi nuclear deal, throwing pact into uncertain terrain
content: US President Trump linked the Saudi nuclear deal to Abraham Accords normalization.  This condition was not previously stated when the agreement was unveiled.  Saudi Arabia maintains its stance on Palestinian statehood progress.  The deal faces scrutiny in Congress and among critics.  This move complicates diplomatic efforts and future negotiations.

title: ‘Must be handled with empathy’: BJP veteran MM Joshi breaks ranks, backs NEET protesters
content: Veteran BJP leader Murli Manohar Joshi supported students protesting the alleged NEET paper leak. He criticized police action against demonstrators as merciless and urged empathy. The government stated its readiness for a debate on the examination system. Opposition parties demanded the Education Minister's resignation and a parliamentary discussion. Prime Minister Modi announced fast-track courts for paper leak offenders.

title: 'Mental strength makes huge difference': Mayank Yadav reflects on comeback
content: Back in India's colours after nearly two years, Mayank returned figures of 2/18 in four overs as India restricted Zimbabwe to 125/7 in the opening T20I of the three-match series at the Harare Sports Club on Thursday.

title: Mamata passes Martyrs' Day street test; marathon begins now
content: The July 21 rally showed that Mamata Banerjee remains the dominant force within a fractured Trinamool. But rebuilding a party stripped of power, organisation and many of its familiar faces will require more than one afternoon of defiance. Experts weigh in on what's the path forward for Mamata Banerjee and TMC.

title: Watch: Sooryavanshi's emotional 'A' celebration steals hearts after world-record fifty
content: Vaibhav Sooryavanshi became the youngest player to score an international T20 fifty. He achieved this remarkable feat against Zimbabwe in Harare on Thursday. The 15-year-old smashed his half-century in just eighteen balls. This explosive innings featured four boundaries and four sixes for India. The celebration was a tribute to his mother, Aarti, whose name starts with 'A'.

title: Oil's $100 comeback: Brent hits a three-month high as Red Sea tensions escalate
content: Crude oil prices surged past one hundred dollars per barrel amid Middle East tensions. Houthi attacks on Saudi oil tankers in the Red Sea caused market concern. This escalation has heightened fears over global energy supply safety and stability. Domestic crude futures also climbed significantly, mirroring the international price rally. Geopolitical risks are now the primary driver for the current oil market surge.

title: UP polls: 'UP ke ladke' together again to take on BJP, but who will show 'bigger heart'?
content: Congress and Samajwadi Party leaders seek a 'big heart' from each other for upcoming UP elections.  Rahul Gandhi assured unity, while UP Congress leaders demand equal status.  Both parties cite past election results to justify their seat claims.  Past alliance failures in Bihar and Haryana highlight potential friction points.  Successful seat-sharing is crucial for the INDIA bloc to challenge the BJP.

title: 1st T20I: Captain Shreyas Iyer ends winless streak as India crush Zimbabwe
content: India secured a seven-wicket victory against Zimbabwe in the first T20I match.  Teenage sensation Vaibhav Sooryavanshi became the youngest player to score a T20I half-century.  Mayank Yadav impressed on his comeback with early wickets for India.  Zimbabwe posted 125 runs after a fighting partnership from their batsmen.  This win marked Shreyas Iyer's first as captain, ending a winless streak.

title: 'Sonam, it's done bro': Salman urges to stop protests after PM Modi ensures action
content: Salman Khan initially supported student protests against the NEET paper leak. He later backed Prime Minister Narendra Modi's promise of strict action. Khan urged students to return home and Sonam Wangchuk to end his hunger strike. Prime Minister Modi announced fast-track courts for paper leak cases. The government aims to safeguard students' interests and punish wrongdoers.

title: Watch: DRDO conducts maiden flight test of long-range Kusha air defence missile
content: India's Defence Research and Development Organisation successfully flight tested the indigenous Kusha missile. This long-range air defence system aims to counter various aerial threats effectively. The missile system is planned as a three-tier architecture with multiple interceptor ranges. Bharat Electronics Limited is the principal industry partner for radar systems integration. Project Kusha forms a key component of India's future national air defence shield.

title: The passenger pigeon’s extinction: How a once-abundant bird vanished forever
content: The passenger pigeon, once North America's most abundant bird, faced extinction rapidly. Industrial hunting and habitat destruction led to its demise. The last pigeon, Martha, died in 1914, marking a significant loss. This event highlighted the dangers of assuming endless natural resources. Its story remains a crucial lesson for modern wildlife protection efforts.

title: Sooryavanshi scripts history, becomes the youngest to score an international fifty
content: Teenage sensation Vaibhav Sooryavanshi faced early struggles in his international T20I debut series. He scored modest totals in his initial three innings against England. However, Sooryavanshi then achieved his first international half-century against Zimbabwe. This remarkable performance made him the youngest player to score a fifty in T20I cricket. His innings secured a victory for India in Harare.

title: 'It will be a tragedy': Ronaldo's retirement could cost Portugal millions, expert warns
content: Portugal's football federation risks millions in revenue and global attention after Cristiano Ronaldo retires. Experts warn of a major commercial and visibility crisis for the FPF without preparation. Ronaldo's immense global brand and social media influence far exceed the national team's current appeal. The federation must develop new strategies to attract sponsors and audiences post-Ronaldo.

title: India's dying rivers: How our cities are choking their lifelines
content: India's expanding cities are encroaching upon rivers, reducing their width and flow. Many rivers now carry more sewage than freshwater, impacting ecosystems and communities. Efforts to clean these waterways face challenges from ongoing pollution and development pressures. The Brahmaputra river faces erosion, while the Ganga struggles with industrial and ritual waste. Protecting river floodplains and managing sewage are crucial for their survival.

title: Trisha Krishnan's surprise appearance at Vijay’s ‘Jana Nayagan’ first-day screening
content: Vijay's final film 'Jana Nayagan' finally released, sparking grand celebrations across Tamil Nadu. Actress Trisha Krishnan attended a screening and was mobbed by enthusiastic fans outside the theatre. Security personnel quickly intervened to safely escort Trisha and her mother to their waiting vehicle. The film 'Jana Nayagan' features a compelling story and a star-studded cast. This release marks a significant moment for fans of the beloved actor.

title: Ashes 2027 schedule out: England to begin Australia showdown on June 18
content: England's cricket board has revealed a busy 2027 home schedule.  The men's and women's Ashes series against Australia will run concurrently.  England will also host Pakistan and New Zealand for white-ball matches.  The Rose Bowl in Southampton will stage a men's Ashes Test for the first time.  The Oval will host the 2027 World Test Championship final.

title: Mayank Yadav scripts a rare record with first-ball wicket in IND vs ZIM T20I
content: Mayank Yadav took a wicket with the first ball of the T20I match. He joined Arshdeep Singh and Hardik Pandya in this elite Indian bowling group. This was Yadav's second time achieving this specific T20I opening delivery dismissal. The review confirmed the wicket after an initial appeal was turned down. This gave India a perfect start to their bowling innings.

title: Inside Arshad Warsi and Maria Goretti’s restored Goa villa
content: Actor Arshad Warsi and wife Maria Goretti have restored a 150-year-old Portuguese-style bungalow in Saligao, North Goa, transforming it into Casa Zen which is a five-bedroom heritage property with vibrant interiors, a swimming pool, solar panels and lush gardens. The home is now available for tourists to rent at Rs 75,000 per night, plus taxes.

title: Middle East crisis: Jaishankar says attacks on civilian shipping can't be tolerated
content: External affairs minister S Jaishankar stressed maritime law adherence and regional cooperation at the East Asia Summit. He called for diplomatic engagement to resolve West Asia crises and ensure safe sea lane navigation. Global conflicts impact the world economy, especially affecting the Global South. India supports a substantive Code of Conduct for the South China Sea. Jaishankar also met with leaders from Oman, the UK, and the Netherlands.

title: 'I hope we can all unite': Bindra urges to 'strengthen education system' amid protests
content: Without directly commenting on the ongoing protests or demands for the removal of Union education minister Dharmendra Pradhan, Bindra said the focus should remain on building a transparent and merit-based education system.

title: Resigned? Here's what labour laws say about final settlement
content: For an employee, the last salary from the previous organisation is not just a payroll entry. It may be needed for rent, relocation expenses, family commitments, education fees, loan repayments or simply to manage the gap before the first salary from the new employer

title: At least 1,200 rescued as torrential rain pounds south Gujarat; PM Modi, Amit Shah review situation
content: One rain-related death reported in Tapi district; IMD issues red alert for Valsad, Navsari, Dang, Surat and Tapi districts for Thursday and Friday; NDRF, SDRF and IAF placed on alert

title: Women’s reservation should be implemented without delimitation: Justice Hariparanthaman
content: If the increase in Lok Sabha seats is based on population, southern States will stand to lose adequate representation in the Lower House, says former Madras High Court judge

title: Kerala University Syndicate scraps ₹2-crore sculpture park project
content: The sculpture park, proposed in the university’s 2025-26 budget by the previous Left-dominant Syndicate, was expected to feature statues of eminent personalities including Karl Marx, Charles Darwin, Socrates, and Rabindranath Tagore

title: Bangladesh PM Tarique Rahman invited to BRICS summit in Delhi
content: Bangladesh’s Minister for Foreign Affairs says the Indian government has invited Mr. Rahman to participate in the summit as a guest

title: Over 6.17 lakh Waqf properties approved on UMEED portal
content: The portal is part of government’s ongoing initiative to digitise and streamline Waqf property records

title: No debate without Pradhan’s resignation, Opposition tells govt. as it hits the streets
content: INDIA bloc closes ranks on paper leaks, rejects discussion offer and carries protest from Parliament to Gandhi Smriti in a show of solidarity with agitating students; in the meeting, it was felt that a debate would offer the govt. a chance to grandstand its way through by leaning on its numerical strength in both Houses of Parliament

title: Pawan calls for enhanced surveillance to combat red sanders smuggling
content: Addressing a review meeting, the Deputy Chief Minister directs officials to dismantle the smuggling networks down to their roots through a zero-tolerance approach

title: 15 of 39 posts in National Testing Agency lie vacant, says Minister of State for Education
content: In a written reply to CPI(M) Rajya Sabha member V. Sivadasan, the Minister says 24 employees who are employed with the organisation have all been appointed on deputation from various institutions

title: ‘Jana Nayagan’ movie review: ‘Thalapathy’ Vijay bids adieu to cinema with an overstuffed crowd-pleaser
content: Vijay’s farewell film is in equal parts a crowd-pleasing tribute and a political manifesto, wrapped within an uneven but engaging masala entertainer

title: Students' disappointment understandable: Tendulkar issues statement on NEET agitation
content: Mr. Tendulkar said he is hopeful of a solution coming through and stressed on the need to promote a culture which rewards honesty and in which merit wins

title: Assam flood: Over 6.43 lakh affected; Central team will visit soon, Shah tells CM
content: Report says 35 revenue circles and 889 villages in the 10 districts are affected; a crop area of 24891.66 hectares also remains submerged

title: Supreme Court permits officials to process applications for setting up non-polluting MSMEs in Taj Trapezium Zone
content: A Bench comprising CJI Surya Kant stated that every proposal should be scrutinised by experts nominated by the Central Empowered Committee and the National Environmental Engineering Research Institute

title: Man dies days after arrest in Thoothukudi; family alleges custodial torture
content: Arunachalam was arrested for illegal sale of liquor bottles; he was released on July 17, after which his family admitted him to Thoothukudi government hospital, where he was being treated in the neurosurgery ward

title: Centre considering lifting restrictions on airport ownership
content: The current rules cap airline ownership in airports and are inferred to similarly restrict airport operators from owning airlines

title: WATCH: CJP Protest gets backing from film stars, writers & musicians
content: The CJP Protest has received support from across India’s entertainment industry. What began as a student-led movement has now drawn backing from film stars, writers, musicians, television personalities and artists, bringing the protests into the national spotlight.

title: Parliament stalled again as Opposition continues to seek Dharmendra Pradhan’s resignation
content: Rajya Sabha functions for less than 10 minutes on Thursday morning before being adjourned twice; Leader of the House J.P. Nadda terms the Opposition’s conduct ‘highly irresponsible’; Opposition members continue to raise slogans in the Lower House, forcing repeated adjournments

title: IndiGo, Air India oppose Adani’s airline entry
content: It is a recipe for conflict of interest as well as squeezing other players; such a consolidation across the value chain not only kills competition but also results in fewer jobs, said an Air India official

title: Watch: India rallies behind the CJP Protest
content: The CJP protest that began as a march in Delhi has rapidly transformed into a nationwide movement. Following police action against CJP protesters and activist Sonam Wangchuk, demonstrations erupted across India, with students, political parties, and civil society groups taking to the streets.

title: Revanth questions BRS’ silence on demand for Dharmendra Pradhan’s resignation
content: Revanth refers to Intermediate examination paper evaluation controversy during the previous BRS regime, defends his governments initiatives and argues another dam would have to be constructed near Medigadda if water has to be stored

title: Nithin Raj case: prime accused surrenders before Crime Branch, remanded
content: The Crime Branch had sought permission to re-arrest Dr. Ram after the Thalassery Principal Sessions Court ordered his release, ruling that investigators had failed to comply with the Supreme Court’s guidelines governing arrests when he was taken into custody in Karnataka

title: Hundreds gather in Kerala’s Kochi to express solidarity with Delhi student protest
content: Participants express solidarity with the ongoing student protests in New Delhi over the alleged NEET question paper leak and condemn police action against the protesters

title: Rift in AMMA triggers fresh row over ‘power group’ in Malayalam cinema
content: Allegation by Shwetha Menon that the ‘power group’ named in Hema Committee report is working against her has triggered a fresh bout of infighting within AMMA even as her rival camp has asked her to divulge the names of those named in the controversial report

title: Sirajudheen Ellathodi to represent Kerala on Centre’s MSME policy panel
content: He will be one of the seven experts from across the country in the committee, which will focus on strengthening financial support systems for small traders, traditional traders and MSME entrepreneurs

title: Dry spell to continue for most parts of A.P. until July 30; overall deficit reaches 53.3%
content: All the districts in Andhra Pradesh, except for SPSR Nellore, have reported deficient rainfall as of July 23; West Godavari reports the sharpest rainfall gap at 76.8%

title: Karnataka Deputy Chief Minister G. Parameshwara writes to Modi seeking replacement of NEET with State CETs
content: The Deputy Chief Minister appeals to the Prime Minister to provide a compensation of at least ₹1 crore to the families of students who ended their lives owing to the NEET paper leak and the “systemic failures” that followed.

title: Delhi High Court agrees to hear PIL seeking probe into Cockroach Janta Party protest
content: A former vice president of the Akhil Bharat Hindu Mahasabha files petition, alleging that the participation of activists and political leaders has raised questions about the nature and objective of the protest

title: 555 cases pending against sitting/former MPs, MLAs in Kerala
content: Court observes that data provided by the High Court administration is in variance with the report by the State Police Chief, which cited 256 pending cases. It directs SPC to correct the statistics

title: Elected representatives in West Bengal panchayat, civic bodies barred from issuing birth, death certificates
content: Police teams conducted searches at municipalities and corporations in several districts of West Bengal as part of an investigation into irregularities in the issuance of birth and death certificates.

title: Canolly Plot in Kerala’s Nilambur to get integrated eco-tourism makeover
content: Renovation work on the suspension bridge across the Chaliyar, connecting the two parts of the Canolly Plot, to be expedited

title: Yuva Morcha to take out protest march to Kerala CM’s residence on July 24
content: It will raise various issues including the PSC examination irregularities and the Congress-led agitation to the Prime Minister’s official residence

title: Tech Mahindra employee protests outside campus over alleged termination after availing leave to care for ailing daughter
content: In response, Tech Mahindra said it had connected with the employee and was reviewing the matter with urgency.

title: Ruling NDA, INDIA bloc MPs face off with opposing protests within Parliament House complex
content: The two groups of MPs stood at the Makar Dwar and shouted slogans; Opposition MPs call for the resignation of the Union Education Minister, and NDA MPs accused the Opposition of politicising the issue

title: 220 teachers in Kerala promoted as high school head teacher or AEO
content: Schools and teachers’ organisations had been raising concerns over the delay in effecting head teacher promotion as around 250 posts were lying vacant

title: Major Ravi seeks to downplay Vismaya Mohanlal’s social media post in support of student protesters in New Delhi
content: Bharatiya Janata Party leader says her viral Instagram post mainly stemmed from compassion rather than politics and she does not have much political education

title: Over two decades after 2003 CAT paper leak, two CBI cases against Ranjit ‘Don’ remain pending in courts
content: The Patna case is at the trial stage, while the proceedings in Delhi case are yet to reach that point

title: A.P.’s first private women’s varsity, govt. degree college for hearing-impaired get Cabinet nod
content: The college to be set up at Vedullapalli in Bapatla district from 2026-27 academic year will initially admit 30 students and offer a B.Com (Computer Applications) along with a certificate course in Artificial Intelligence

title: Gold mine at Jonnagiri renamed as Swarnagiri Gold Mine
content: Spread over 597.820 hectares in Tuggali mandal, the mine primarily produces gold, along with silver, copper, lead and zinc

title: Kerala government launches women-only ‘pink bus’ service
content: The project, which has been launched in Thiruvananthapuram, will be extended to other districts soon, and the routes and timings will be determined after assessing the requirements of the passengers

title: Child rights panel talks to children assaulted by teacher in Kerala
content: Alleged assault on students by a physical education teacher at Government Higher Secondary School, Kanjikode, in Palakkad, Kerala

title: PNB partners with A.P. to set up Quantum Finance Innovation Hub in Amaravati
content: It will focus on making digital banking services more secure, efficient and customer-friendly through adoption of advanced technologies, says PNB Director and CEO Ashok Chandra

title: KPSC Veterinary Officer recruitment under scanner as corruption allegations surface
content: The protesters claim that an alleged sting operation had exposed a cash-for-jobs racket, in which an agent was heard demanding ₹80 lakh for securing a Veterinary Officer post, with ₹40 lakh to be paid in advance.

title: "Dharmendra Pradhan Should Join Us At Jantar Mantar": Abhijeet Dipke To NDTV
content: "I don't understand why is it such a big problem to sack one education minister? One education minister cannot be more important than lakhs of students," Abhijeet Dipke told NDTV

title: Detentions, Demolitions Will Worsen Situation: Omar Abdullah After Cop Killing
content: Over 2,000 terror suspects were detained, and the houses of families of two terrorists were demolished in Anantnag last night.

title: Saadia Zahidi Becomes First Woman Chief Of International Aviation Body
content: In a release, IATA said Zahidi would be the ninth Director General and the first woman appointed to hold the position and would take charge from November 1.

title: Mobile Internet Suspended Around Jantar Mantar, Cops Say No Crackdown Tonight
content: Section 163 of the Bharatiya Nagarik Suraksha Sanhita, which bans large gatherings, has also been imposed outside Union Home Minister Amit Shah's residence, with police issuing warnings in the area.

title: Eating Fire For Breakfast: Hungry Donkeys Turn Firefighters In Spain
content: According to a report by CNN, instead of carrying hoses, donkeys are eating their way through the dry vegetation that fuels wildfires.

title: CJP Protest LIVE Updates: No Plan To Crack Down On Student Protest Tonight, Says Delhi Police
content: CJP Jantar Mantar protest LIVE: The Cockroach Janta Party (CJP) protesters are continuing their sit-in at Jantar Mantar.

title: "Students Must Not Be Met With Force": Group Of IIM Women Alumni
content: They also urged the government to meet the students and listen to them, and hold Dharmendra Pradhan accountable "for this failure by calling for his resignation".

title: 'Forum Cynically Exploited By Pak Again': India On Ishaq Dar's Remarks At ASEAN
content: Pakistan is once again "cynically exploiting" a multilateral forum to amplify falsehoods and peddle state-sponsored disinformation, India said

title: India Develops First Indigenous 350 Kg Expendable Turbojet Engine
content: GTRE had selected Azad Engineering as its industry partner for the manufacturing and assembly of the engine.

title: Rahul Gandhi, Opposition MPs Protest At Gandhi Smriti To Support Students
content: Rahul Gandhi said India's students are not alone and that the entire Opposition stands with them and their demands.

title: "Sonam, It's Done, Bro": Salman Khan Urges Wangchuk To End Fast, Asks Students To Go Back
content: Sonam Wangchuk has been on hunger strike since June 28, demanding accountability over paper leaks and reforms in the education system.

title: India Tests Kusha Long-Range Surface-To-Air Defence Missile
content: Defence Minister Rajnath Singh said it signifies a "giant leap" in air defence capability of the nation.

title: Students' Concerns Genuine, Must Be Handled With Empathy: BJP Veteran MM Joshi
content: "I fervently hope that this is not treated merely as a law-and-order issue to be tackled through the use of force," former BJP chief Murli Manohar Joshi said

title: How To Poison Someone, Burn Body: Bengaluru Man Asks AI Before Triple Murders
content: Investigators found that the accused had used Google Gemini to create hypothetical scenarios and seek information related to committing crimes.

title: Opinion: Opinion | A New US-Israel Defence Bill Has Opened An Unexpected Door For India
content: The phrase "integration of the US and Israeli militaries" has become popular because it captures attention. It also obscures the more important story.

title: US-Saudi Nuclear Agreement Hinges On Riyadh Recognising Israel: Trump
content: The deal to establish a civilian nuclear program in Saudi Arabia "will be approved, but is totally subject to Saudi Arabia joining the very respected and successful Abraham Accords," Trump said.

title: Secret Service Agent Suspended For 'Leaking' JD Vance's Travel Details
content: The agency's internal affairs component has opened an investigation into it; however, it is unclear if the agent could face administrative punishment or criminal charges.

title: Delhi University Rs 26,000, Allahabad Rs 3,000: Students Question Fee Disparity
content: "Can children of farmers like us afford such high fees? This is a fight for educational reform, and the government must take it seriously," a student at Jantar Mantar said
